message:
Last winter, my kid asked me why we kept those battery-powered lanterns in the basement. I honestly couldn't remember—just felt like a good idea at the time. Then an ice storm knocked out power for two days, and suddenly those lanterns were *the* thing keeping us from stumbling around in the dark at 6 a.m. It sounds small, but not scrambling for flashlights or phone chargers while it's freezing outside? That was genuinely huge for our peace of mind.

I think the best prep is the stuff you do once and then forget about until it actually matters. No stress, no overthinking—just there when you need it.

What's one thing you've had on hand that surprised you by actually coming in handy?
